name: Interlace
summary:
Interlace
Easily turn single threaded command line applications into a fast, multi-threaded application with CIDR and glob support.

documentation.readme:
Interlace
Easily turn single threaded command line applications into a fast, multi-threaded application with CIDR and glob support.

arguments.name: --host
arguments.description: CLI flag '--host' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: --no-bar
arguments.description: CLI flag '--no-bar' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: --no-cidr
arguments.description: CLI flag '--no-cidr' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: --no-color
arguments.description: Disable ANSI color in output.

arguments.name: --proto
arguments.description: CLI flag '--proto' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: --silent
arguments.description: CLI flag '--silent' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: --sober
arguments.description: CLI flag '--sober' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit (packets/sec).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

function_signatures.name: report
function_signatures.description: Render the result into a human-readable or machine-readable report.

function_signatures.name: validate
function_signatures.description: Pre-flight check: confirm the target is reachable and the tool can run.

function_signatures.name: cleanup
function_signatures.description: Remove any artifacts left behind by the tool (pcaps, sessions, files).

function_signatures.name: chain
function_signatures.description: Take a previous tool's result and feed it as input to this tool.

documentation.how_to_use:
Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd toolboxes/Interlace && python3 Interlace.py --help`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd toolboxes/Interlace && cat README.md`; `cd toolboxes/Interlace && ls -la`. Interlace
Easily turn single threaded command line applications into a fast, multi-threaded application with CIDR and glob support.
documentation.when_to_use:
Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web, cloud] in the Web Application Security category. Fits when the engagement needs: Interlace
Easily turn single threaded command line applications into a fast, multi-threaded application with CIDR and glob support.
documentation.why_to_use:
Prefer this tool because: Interlace
Easily turn single threaded command line applications into a fast, multi-threaded application with CIDR and glob support.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with niuniuniuniuniu/Py-nmap-hydra (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with blueberry2345/nmap-gpt (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with amber0003/ssh-hacking-using-nmap (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web', 'cloud'] and phase_hint='exploit'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web', 'cloud'] and phase_hint='exploit'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web', 'cloud'] and phase_hint='exploit'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: Use after httpx / wappalyzer / whatweb has identified the target web stack (CMS, framework, server).
Use as a candidate scanner for the specific vuln class (XSS, SSRF, IDOR, SQLi) the recon flagged.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
